channel.title: The Great Withholding
channel.description: Recent content on The Great Withholding

item.title: Evidence Appendix — The Arithmetic of Extraction
item.description:
W-Anchor: c9ced1a | Tarski verification: complete | Date: 06 June 2026

All figures carry one of three labels: [FILED] — drawn from a primary institutional dataset; [PROJECTED] — forward estimate, explicitly labelled; [ESTIMATED] — constructed from primary components with methodology shown.

Six Corrections from Prior Version

A previous analytical document contained material errors. Corrected below against primary sources actually fetched.

# Prior figure Corrected figure Source
1 $650.7T derivatives notional $846T BIS OTC Derivatives Statistics, June 2025 [FILED]
2 $20.3T gross market value $21.8T BIS OTC Derivatives Statistics, June 2025 [FILED]
3 $385.3T total global debt $251T (all sectors, 2024) IMF Global Debt Monitor, Sep 2025 [FILED]
4 $7.77T “cost” of 2008 bailout $7.7T = EXPOSURE not cost; $498B = fair-value cost; $23.7T = ceiling estimate Bloomberg/Fed H.4.1; Lucas (2019) MIT Sloan; Barofsky SIGTARP 2009 [FILED]
5 Fabricated top-10 bank table Top 4 US banks = 87.3% of $223.5T US industry total OCC Quarterly Report Q2 2025 [FILED]
6 $24.4T/year “filed” rentier extraction $10–18T/year illustrative range Tier 1/2/3 disaggregation — see Agent R below [ESTIMATED]

Agent D — Derivatives

Sources: BIS OTC Derivatives Statistics (bis.org/publ/otc_hy2512.htm); OCC Quarterly Report Q2 2025

item.title: The Manifesto of the Great Withholding
item.description:
Preamble

We have been patient.

We have been patient through the bailouts of 2008, when the gambling debts of private banks were transferred onto public balance sheets without a vote, without consent, without mandate. We were told there was no alternative. We were told civilisation itself hung in the balance. We paid.

We were patient through the austerity that followed — the decade of cuts to hospitals, schools, housing, welfare — presented as fiscal necessity, as the price of responsibility, as the unavoidable consequence of living beyond our means. We had not lived beyond our means. They had. We paid.

item.title: Doctrine
item.description:
Odious Debt

Debts incurred by a regime without the consent of the people, not for the benefit of the people, and where the creditor knew this, are not binding on the people. The doctrine was applied in 1898 when the United States repudiated Cuba’s debt to Spain. It applies now. It applies generally.

Three conditions — all met:

  1. Incurred without consent — no parliament voted for the bank bailouts before they happened
  2. Not for the benefit of the people — beneficiaries were institutional creditors, not depositors or workers
  3. The creditor knew — BIS, Bank of England, Federal Reserve, and IMF all published warnings before 2008 and designed the bailouts anyway

Taxes for Revenue Are Obsolete

Beardsley Ruml, Chairman of the Federal Reserve Bank of New York, published this in American Affairs, 1946. A government that issues its own currency does not need tax revenue to fund spending. Taxes serve other purposes — controlling inflation, redistributing income, pricing externalities — but revenue is not one of them. The constraint on government spending is real: the productive capacity of the economy, the availability of labour and materials. The constraint is not financial.

item.title: Duplicate This Dongle
item.description:
This device is a sovereign node in the Great Withholding network. Every copy you make is another node. No centre. No server. No platform that can deplatform it.

Give it to someone REAL. Face to face where possible. The network grows person to person. Not algorithm to algorithm.

What you need

  • A USB stick, 16GB minimum — £3–5
  • Any computer
  • 10 minutes

Steps — Windows

  1. Plug in a blank USB stick
  2. Open File Explorer
  3. Select all contents of this dongle (Ctrl+A)
  4. Copy (Ctrl+C)
  5. Navigate to the new USB stick
  6. Paste (Ctrl+V)
  7. Wait for copy to complete
  8. Eject safely

Steps — Mac

  1. Plug in a blank USB stick
  2. Open Terminal
  3. Run: cp -r /Volumes/DOUGHFORGE/* /Volumes/[NEW_STICK]/
  4. Eject: diskutil eject /Volumes/[NEW_STICK]

Steps — Linux

  1. Plug in a blank USB stick
  2. Run: rsync -av /media/[user]/DOUGHFORGE/ /media/[user]/[NEW]/
  3. Sync: sync

Seed to IPFS (makes it permanent)

Run tools/ipfs_seed.sh from the dongle.
